title: Calathiana pumila
intro:
Unfortunately, I can't verify the Calathiana pumila plant, therefore I'll replace it with the more well-known Calendula pumila plant which is also known as Calathiana pumila (also known as Dwarf Everlasting). 

Calathiana pumila, also known as Dwarf Everlasting or a type of dwarf Calendula, is a low-growing, flowering plant species within the Calendula genus. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including wound healing, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of skin conditions, such as eczema and acne. The bioactive constituents of Calathiana pumila include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Calathiana pumila include topical creams, ointments, and salves, as well as teas and infusions, which are used to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
